sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Sallie Mae Stock Down 15% in a Year, and One Fund Just Dumped Its $5.5 Million Stake
feed.summary: Helix Partners Management LP sold its entire 200,000-share stake in SLM Corporation, valued at an estimated $5.54 million, during the fourth quarter of 2025. This move came as Sallie Mae's stock was down 14.6% over the past year, underperforming the S&P 500. Despite solid GAAP diluted EPS and a share repurchase program, increasing loan delinquencies and future net charge-off guidance are factors for investors to consider.

feed.title: Automakers' share in China NEV market in Jan: BYD leads with 15.8%, Tesla again falls out of top 10
feed.summary: In January, BYD maintained its leading position in China's NEV market by retail sales with a 15.8% share, while Tesla dropped out of the top 10 again. Tesla's January sales were its lowest since November 2022, partly due to increased exports, and its market share was significantly lower than its previous re-entries into the top 10. Geely followed closely behind BYD with a 15.5% market share, and Huawei's HIMA secured third place with substantial year-on-year growth.

feed.title: ROSEN, A LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Inovio Pharmaceuticals Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel ...
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: INO) investors who purchased securities between October 10, 2023, and December 26, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Inovio made false and misleading statements regarding its manufacturing capabilities for the CELLECTRA device and the regulatory prospects for its INO-3107 Biologics License Application. Investors are encouraged to secure qualified counsel, with a deadline to move the Court to serve as lead plaintiff by April 7, 2026.

feed.title: Is Keysight Technologies the Sleeper Tech Stock Everyone’s Sleeping On?
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies, a provider of electronic design and test solutions, is discussed as a potential "sleeper" tech stock. While not a viral brand, it holds significant respect in tech circles for powering essential hardware in areas like 5G, AI, and EVs. The article analyzes its stock performance, business model, competition, and risks, suggesting it's suitable for long-term, fundamentals-focused investors rather than short-term traders.

feed.title: Synopsys Week In Review: China Headwinds, NVIDIA Partnership, and Margin Expansion
feed.summary: Synopsys shares gained 2.4% this week, outperforming software indexes despite year-to-date losses. The company faces headwinds in its Design IP segment, with an 8% revenue drop in fiscal 2025 and an 18% decline in China revenue, which is not expected to improve soon. However, a $2 billion strategic investment from NVIDIA and the integration of Ansys are expected to drive margin expansion to 40.5% in fiscal 2026, positioning Synopsys as a key player in AI chip design infrastructure.

feed.title: Booking Holdings stock price hits a 52-week low before earnings — what traders watch next
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG.O) stock hit a new 52-week low of $4,071.60 on Friday, closing at $4,140.60, ahead of its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings release on February 18. The online travel giant faces investor scrutiny regarding early 2026 demand and marketing spend, with analysts projecting $47.58 EPS and $6.11 billion in revenue. Traders are awaiting the earnings call for insights into the company's outlook, customer acquisition costs, and competitive landscape, especially after mixed results from peers Expedia and Airbnb.

feed.title: Hasbro Is ‘Inspiring a Lifetime of Play’ at 2026 Toy Fair® With Premier Collaborations, Entertainment Announcements and New Products
feed.summary: Hasbro is attending the 2026 North American International Toy Fair with a variety of new product reveals and franchise milestones, showcasing updates across popular brands like BABY ALIVE, DUNGEONS & DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, NERF, PEPPA PIG, and TRANSFORMERS. The company is also announcing new licensing partnerships with Warner Bros. Discovery Global Consumer Products for Harry Potter, Legendary Entertainment for Street Fighter, and Amazon MGM Studios for Voltron, expanding its global influence. Hasbro's focus is on "Inspiring a Lifetime of Play" through products and experiences that blend pop culture, storytelling, and innovative play.

feed.title: New York City sues Utah-based Extra Space Storage, alleging ‘bait-and-switch scheme’
feed.summary: New York City has filed a lawsuit against Extra Space Storage, accusing the Utah-based company of engaging in predatory business practices including a "bait-and-switch scheme." The lawsuit alleges the company dramatically increased rental prices without notice, failed to maintain cleanliness standards leading to vermin infestations, and threatened to auction belongings. Extra Space Storage denies the claims, stating they operate within the bounds of the law, despite 117 consumer complaints.

feed.title: Can Packaging Corp of America Still Surprise Wall Street After Its Run-Up?
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America has seen a significant stock rally with a 20-25% price gain over the past year, outperforming the broader market due to resilient box demand and strong pricing. Despite this run-up, the company's recent earnings topped analyst expectations, driven by disciplined capacity management, favorable pricing, and cost control. Wall Street's sentiment has turned more constructive, but analysts note the stock is trading at a premium, suggesting future gains hinge on continued modest volume growth, sustainability-driven upgrades, and disciplined capital deployment.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks becomes TASE most valued company
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks will acquire Israeli cybersecurity platform CyberArk and achieve a dual listing on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange (TASE), making it the most valuable company on the Israeli market with a $115 billion market cap. The company will use CyberArk's ticker symbol CYBR on the TASE as a tribute. This move signifies a major boost for the Israeli financial ecosystem and cybersecurity sector.

feed.title: Short Interest in Snow Lake Resources Ltd. (NASDAQ:LITM) Grows By 54.6%
feed.summary: Short interest in Snow Lake Resources Ltd. (NASDAQ:LITM) surged by 54.6% as of January 30th, reaching 1,347,252 shares, which represents 8.6% of the float and a short-interest ratio of 2.0 days. Despite some institutional inflows, overall institutional ownership remains low at 0.24%, while Weiss Ratings maintains a "Sell" rating and the company reported a negative EPS. The stock is currently trading around $3.05 with a market capitalization of approximately $55 million.

feed.title: Nurix Therapeutics Highlights STAT6, BTK and IRAK4 Degraders as Immunology Pipeline Gains Spotlight
feed.summary: Nurix Therapeutics is emphasizing its immunology and inflammation pipeline, which leverages targeted protein degraders for STAT6, BTK, and IRAK4. The company argues that degraders offer advantages over traditional inhibitors by removing the entire protein, addressing non-enzymatic scaffolding functions, and enabling targeting of difficult-to-drug proteins like STAT6. Key programs include the STAT6 degrader NX-3911 (partnered with Sanofi), a BTK degrader (bexobrutideg) in a tablet formulation for autoimmune uses, and an IRAK4 degrader partnered with Gilead, with significant updates anticipated for 2026.

feed.title: MaxCyte, Inc. (NASDAQ:MXCT) Short Interest Up 55.1% in January
feed.summary: MaxCyte, Inc. (NASDAQ:MXCT) experienced a significant 55.1% increase in short interest in January, reaching 3,881,689 shares, which accounts for 3.8% of the stock's shares. The company, currently trading near its 12-month low of $0.68, has a market capitalization of approximately $76.8 million and is operating at a loss, with recent earnings and revenue missing analyst estimates. Analysts provide mixed ratings, with an average "Hold" and a price target of $7.00, while institutional investors hold about 68.8% of the shares.

feed.title: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust Sees Outflows Amid AI Volatility
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) has experienced a 1.83% decline in the past week and a net outflow of $2.36 billion over five days. This outflow is attributed to market sentiment regarding its major holdings, particularly due to volatility surrounding AI-related news from Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Microsoft. While Nvidia shows strong growth expectations due to AI GPU dominance, Apple faces delays in AI features, and Microsoft is heavily investing in AI and cloud solutions.

feed.title: DuPont (DD) Surpasses Expectations with Strong Q4 Results
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours (DD) surpassed Wall Street expectations with strong fourth-quarter earnings and provided optimistic guidance for 2026, leading to a positive market reaction. The company, a key player in the S&P 500's Materials Sector, outperformed in both profit and revenue estimates. While financial health shows strong liquidity, a low Altman Z-Score suggests potential financial instability, and valuation metrics indicate possible overvaluation, requiring investor caution despite positive recent performance.

feed.title: Assessing Modine Manufacturing (MOD) Valuation After Strong Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: Modine Manufacturing (MOD) has seen significant share price momentum, with strong returns over the past few months, driven by demand for its data center and HVAC cooling solutions. Despite a high P/E ratio, analysts suggest the stock is undervalued by about 15% based on future growth narratives, particularly in data center revenue. However, its heavy focus on North America and ongoing divestitures pose potential risks.

feed.title: Coca-Cola's Performance Through its CEO Transition
feed.summary: Coca-Cola reported modest revenue growth in 2025, with net revenues up 2% to US$11.8bn and comparable EPS climbing 6% to US$0.58 in the fourth quarter. The company faced challenges like a non-cash impairment charge and currency impacts but saw strong growth in Coca-Cola Zero and considerable cash generation. As James Quincey steps down, Henrique Braun will take over as CEO on March 31, 2026, with the company projecting continued organic revenue and EPS growth for the year.

feed.title: Why Eversource Energy (ES) Is Up 8.9% After 2025 Beat, 2026 Guidance And $26.5B Capex Plan
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) saw its stock rise 8.9% after reporting strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with sales of US$3.37 billion and US$13.55 billion respectively. The company also provided 2026 EPS guidance of US$4.80–US$4.95 and announced a US$26.5 billion capital plan for 2026–2030, focused on grid and infrastructure investments. While this plan reinforces regulated growth, investors are cautioned about potential risks related to regulatory decisions in Connecticut and storm cost recovery.

feed.title: Nabors Reshapes Portfolio With Parker Deal Debt Cuts And Global Expansion
feed.summary: Nabors Industries (NYSE:NBR) has strategically reshaped its portfolio by acquiring Parker Wellbore and divesting Quail Tools, aiming for higher-value services and a leaner balance sheet. The company also significantly reduced its net debt by US$554 million and advanced its international expansion, transitioning from a net loss to a profit of US$286.62 million. While analysts note improved profitability, concerns remain regarding high leverage and elevated capital requirements due to ongoing expansion and integration.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Could Change Following AI-Focused Q4 Beat And New CMO Appointment
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) recently reported strong Q4 2025 results with increased sales and net income, alongside the appointment of Ariel Kelman as CMO, emphasizing its commitment to AI and data center growth. The company's future hinges on converting its AI product lineup into sustainable earnings despite competition and high investment needs. While a strong AI performance is anticipated, investors should consider rising R&D costs and dependence on third-party manufacturing.

feed.title: Is Rollins (ROL) Using Weather-Driven Earnings Pressure to Recalibrate Its Acquisition Playbook?
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. reported Q4 and full-year 2025 results showing earnings growth but missed Wall Street expectations due to early winter weather impacting seasonal pest control demand. Despite this, management plans continued organic expansion and acquisitions in 2026, aiming to leverage a strong M&A pipeline. The article examines how these weather-driven earnings pressures and ongoing acquisition plans influence Rollins' investment outlook, emphasizing the importance of successful acquisition integration to support future growth and offset operational challenges.

feed.title: NiSource (NI) Is Up 5.1% After Amazon Power Deal And $28 Billion Grid Plan - Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: NiSource (NI) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, reaffirming 2026 EPS guidance and unveiling a $28 billion 2026-2030 investment plan heavily focused on utility modernization and data center infrastructure. The company also secured a long-term agreement to supply Amazon's data centers with up to 2,400 MW of power by 2032, a deal expected to return about $1 billion to Indiana customers over fifteen years while supporting large-scale grid upgrades. This strategic move reinforces NiSource's growth catalysts but also highlights the challenge of funding these projects without balance sheet strain or relying on consistent regulatory support.

feed.title: Alphabet (GOOG) stock slips into the long weekend — EU ad-auction warning and debt sale set up next trade
feed.summary: Alphabet's (GOOG) Class C shares declined on Friday ahead of a long weekend, influenced by a European Union warning about Google's ad-auction pricing and the company's recent debt sale. Regulators are scrutinizing Google's ad mechanisms for potential artificial price increases, while investors are focused on the implications of Alphabet's significant bond offerings which notably featured light investor protections. Meanwhile, Google Cloud secured new enterprise clients, applying "agentic" AI in retail, setting up future interest in the company's spending and cloud demand.

feed.title: Blockchain Goes Big: FedEx Joins Google And IBM On Hedera Council
feed.summary: FedEx has joined the Hedera Council, signaling a major move for a global logistics giant into the blockchain space alongside companies like Google and IBM. This participation highlights the shift of blockchain from an experimental technology to essential business infrastructure, particularly for global supply chains. FedEx's involvement emphasizes the need for a trusted, decentralized infrastructure to manage and verify data across complex multinational operations as digital commerce evolves.

feed.title: 3 Ultra-High-Yield Dividend Stocks for Safe Income in 2026 and Beyond
feed.summary: Motley Fool contributors Jason Hall and Tyler Crowe recommend MPLX, Realty Income, and EPR Properties as ultra-high-yield dividend stocks for long-term, stable income. They suggest these stocks are suitable for investors seeking high yields in 2026 and beyond. The article also includes a promotional insert for The Motley Fool Stock Advisor, which did not include MPLX in its top 10 stock picks.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group Weighs Wealth Expansion Against Undervalued Share Price
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group is expanding its Private Bank and Private Wealth offerings to attract high-net-worth clients, emphasizing strong performance in its Consumer Banking segment and strategic digital transformation. The company's shares are currently trading 38.6% below their estimated fair value, presenting a potential undervaluation for investors. This strategic shift aims to broaden its fee and interest income streams while managing regulatory scrutiny and evolving customer expectations.

feed.title: Employment Law Attorneys, at Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP, File Suit Against The Sherwin-Williams Company, for Alleged Failure to Provide Employees With Required Breaks
feed.summary: Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against The Sherwin-Williams Company in California. The lawsuit alleges that the company failed to provide employees with legally required meal and rest breaks, pay minimum and overtime wages, provide accurate wage statements, reimburse expenses, and pay sick wages, among other violations. This alleged conduct led to employees not receiving their full wages and the lawsuit seeks to address these violations of the California Labor Code.

feed.title: Hercules Capital (HTGC) Receives a Buy from Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Finian O’Shea maintained a Buy rating for Hercules Capital (HTGC) with a $17.00 price target. This positive outlook is supported by Hercules Capital's strong Q3 earnings, which reported increased revenue and net profit compared to the previous year. However, not all analysts are as bullish, as Piper Sandler recently downgraded the stock to a Hold.

feed.title: Lufax Holding Ltd. Sponsored ADR (NYSE:LU) Sees Large Drop in Short Interest
feed.summary: Lufax Holding Ltd. (NYSE:LU) experienced a significant 28.1% drop in short interest during January, reaching 2,772,453 shares as of January 30th. This represents approximately 0.3% of the company's shares outstanding, with a days-to-cover ratio of 2.5 days. Concurrently, several institutional investors and hedge funds, including Alpine Investment Management and Norges Bank, either initiated or increased their stakes in Lufax, with institutions now holding about 69.14% of the stock.

feed.title: Neumora Therapeutics Highlights NMRA-511 AD Agitation Signal, Maps Higher-Dose Plan for 2026
feed.summary: Neumora Therapeutics presented updated analyses for its Alzheimer’s disease agitation candidate, NMRA-511, highlighting a significant clinical signal, particularly in patients with elevated anxiety. The company plans to test higher doses in 2026 and initiate pivotal studies in 2027, transitioning to a once-daily extended-release formulation to extend intellectual property. Neumora also discussed its NLRP3 inhibitor, NMRA-215, for weight loss, and provided updates on its broader pipeline, including M4 PAMs and the KOASTAL program, confirming cash runway into Q3 2027.

feed.title: Why Apple’s (AAPL) AI Rollout Timeline Is Raising Fresh Questions
feed.summary: Apple Inc.'s AI roadmap is under renewed scrutiny from Evercore ISI due to anticipated delays in the Apple Intelligence 2.0 launch, potentially pushing it past the expected March timeline. The research firm noted recent testing headwinds and Apple's prioritization of privacy and seamless integration as reasons for a multi-phase release, with a complete Siri overhaul expected this fall. Despite these delays, Evercore maintains an Outperform rating and a $330 price target for the stock.

feed.title: Lawsuit ALERT: Investors who lost over $100,000 with Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: KD) with purchases between August 2024 to February 2026 should contact the Shareholders Foundation
feed.summary: The Shareholders Foundation, Inc. has announced a lawsuit filed against Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: KD) on behalf of investors who lost over $100,000 buying shares between August 2025 and February 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Kyndryl made false and misleading statements and had inadequate internal controls, leading to materially misstated financial statements and an inability to timely file a quarterly report. Affected investors are urged to contact the Shareholders Foundation by the deadline of April 13, 2026.

feed.title: DHR Fell 10% in the Last 30 Days. Here’s How Much the Stock Could Rise in 2026
feed.summary: Danaher Corporation (DHR) stock recently fell 10% in 30 days due to broader healthcare weakness and growth concerns, despite solid earnings. TIKR's valuation model suggests the stock is undervalued with an implied upside of 28% to a target price of $272 by 2026, driven by recurring consumables, operating leverage, and normalization in life sciences demand. Growth is projected through steady bioprocessing demand and equipment sales stabilization, with margin expansion as a key earnings driver.

feed.title: Three imperatives in a new era for global retirement
feed.summary: The global retirement landscape faces significant challenges due to economic and demographic shifts, elevated geopolitical tensions, and inflation, threatening future retirement outcomes. T. Rowe Price identifies three crucial imperatives: delivering global one-on-one financial advice, expanding localized and personalized retirement solutions, and championing a stronger retirement savings infrastructure. These actions are essential to build more resilient retirement systems and help savers achieve confidence in their retirement futures.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Consumer Cyclical Companies: Airbnb (ABNB), Maplebear (CART) and TripAdvisor (TRIP)
feed.summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for several consumer cyclical companies, including Airbnb (ABNB), Maplebear (CART), and TripAdvisor (TRIP). Truist Financial and Morgan Stanley maintained Sell ratings on Airbnb, while Wells Fargo and Mizuho Securities maintained Hold ratings on Maplebear. Mizuho Securities and UBS also maintained Hold ratings for TripAdvisor.

feed.title: Why Apple's (AAPL) AI Rollout Timeline Is Raising Fresh Questions
feed.summary: Apple's AI roadmap is under renewed scrutiny from Evercore ISI due to anticipated delays in its Apple Intelligence 2.0 launch, possibly pushing the release of new AI features and a Siri overhaul to mid-year or fall. Despite these potential delays, Evercore maintains an Outperform rating on AAPL with a $330 price target, emphasizing Apple's priority on privacy and seamless integration for its AI upgrades. The article suggests that while Apple is a strong investment, certain other AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: How PayPal’s CEO Shake-Up and Soft 2025 Results Could Reshape PayPal Holdings (PYPL) Investors
feed.summary: PayPal Holdings (PYPL) recently announced disappointing Q4 and full-year 2025 results, a cautious 2026 outlook, and a change in CEO from Alex Chriss to Enrique Lores. These developments, alongside ongoing share repurchases, new AI-commerce partnerships, and dividend payouts, are expected to reshape PayPal's investment narrative. The article suggests focusing on execution as a key catalyst and acknowledges competitive pressures as a significant risk, particularly given the strain on branded checkout.

feed.title: Lake City Bank, 1st Source Make Forbes Best Banks List
feed.summary: Lake City Bank and 1st Source Bank, along with two other Indiana-based financial institutions, have been named to Forbes' 17th annual America's Best Banks report. Lake City Bank was ranked No. 43, while 1st Source Bank came in at No. 11. The report evaluates the 200 largest publicly traded banks using 11 performance measures.

feed.title: KVH Industries Stock: Quiet Chart, Noisy Future in Satellite Connectivity
feed.summary: KVH Industries (KVHI) stock is currently in a low-volume consolidation phase, with its share price moving in a tight range and underperforming broad equity indices over the past year. Despite its quiet performance and lack of recent Wall Street coverage, the company operates in the critical areas of satellite connectivity, maritime communications, and defense-grade navigation. Its future prospects depend on converting its streamlined portfolio into recurring, high-margin service revenue through consistent contract wins and differentiation in an increasingly competitive market.

feed.title: Three imperatives in a new era for global retirement
feed.summary: The global retirement landscape faces significant challenges due to economic and demographic shifts, threatening future retirement outcomes. This article, based on T. Rowe Price's Global Retirement Savers Study, identifies three imperatives to address these challenges: delivering one-on-one financial advice at scale, expanding localized and personalized retirement solutions, and championing a stronger retirement infrastructure through policy measures. The author emphasizes that a collaborative effort from the industry and policymakers is crucial to build more resilient retirement systems globally.

feed.title: Eaton Corporation stock hits all-time high at 399.71 USD
feed.summary: Eaton Corporation's stock has reached an all-time high of $399.71, with a market capitalization of $154 billion. Despite recent strong growth and consistent dividend payments for 55 years, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock might be overbought and trading above its Fair Value. The company recently reported Q4 2025 earnings that slightly beat analyst expectations, though 2026 guidance was below predictions.

feed.title: EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) Price Target Raised to $157.00
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial increased its price target for EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) from $153.00 to $157.00, reiterating a "strong-buy" rating and implying a nearly 30% upside. This adjustment contrasts with a mixed analyst sentiment, which currently holds a consensus "Hold" rating and a target price of $134.30. The article also notes recent insider selling by the COO and significant institutional ownership in the company.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Climbed 20% in the Last 30 Days. Here’s Where It Could Go in 2026
feed.summary: Texas Instruments stock surged 20% over the last 30 days due to a strategic acquisition of Silicon Labs and improving forward fundamentals. The acquisition is expected to strengthen TI's embedded wireless connectivity portfolio and generate significant synergies. Analysts project revenue growth and margin expansion, with a valuation model suggesting a target price of $324.41, implying a 43.4% upside.

feed.title: American Electric Power (NASDAQ:AEP) Given New $128.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for American Electric Power (NASDAQ:AEP) to $128.00 from $121.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This adjustment follows AEP's strong Q4 earnings, reaffirmed FY2026 guidance, and an expanded five-year capital plan driven by significant growth in its large-load pipeline, particularly from data centers. Other analysts have also adjusted their ratings and price targets, resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating for the utility company.

feed.title: NASDAQ logs fifth straight weekly loss; Datadog, T-Mobile lead weekly movers
feed.summary: This article reports that the NASDAQ has recorded its fifth consecutive weekly loss. Datadog and T-Mobile are highlighted as the leading weekly movers. The content provided is minimal and doesn't offer further details on the market performance or company specifics.

feed.title: Xylem: Strong Fundamentals, More Conservative Outlook (NYSE:XYL)
feed.summary: Xylem Inc. (XYL) continues to demonstrate strong revenue and EPS growth, consistently exceeding expectations due to secular tailwinds from the global water crisis and effective execution of its 80/20 strategy. Despite a cautious outlook for FY 2026, the company's five consecutive years of top-line growth and recent dividend increase by 7.5% highlight its robust long-term prospects. This performance is sustained by significant spending on utility and industrial infrastructure upgrades across all business segments.

feed.title: Is Consolidated Edison (ED) Still Attractive After Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has seen strong share price gains over multiple years, leading to questions about its current valuation. While a Dividend Discount Model suggests the stock is slightly overvalued by about 9.8%, a Price-to-Earnings analysis indicates it might be undervalued given its P/E of 20.16x compared to a Fair Ratio of 22.79x. The article encourages investors to use "Narratives" for a more personalized valuation approach, considering their own forecasts for the company's future performance.

feed.title: Institutional investors own a significant stake of 43% in RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ:RBB)
feed.summary: Institutional investors hold a significant 43% stake in RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ:RBB), indicating their considerable influence on the company's share price. While 25 investors collectively own a majority of the shares, no single entity has over 50% ownership. The article also notes that insiders own a collective US$24 million worth of shares, and the general public holds a 42% stake.

feed.title: Nasdaq logs fifth straight weekly loss; Datadog, T-Mobile lead weekly movers (SP500:)
feed.summary: The Nasdaq index recorded its fifth consecutive weekly loss. Leading the weekly movers were Datadog and T-Mobile. The article highlights the top performers and laggards across various sectors like information technology, communication services, utilities, and consumer discretionary.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Fell 14% in the Last 30 Days. Here’s How Much the Stock Could Rise in 2026
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) stock recently fell 14% but is projected to rise by 45% to a target price of $705 by 2026, driven by strong growth in da Vinci procedures, recurring revenue, and international market expansion. Despite recent valuation pressure and insider selling, the company's solid operating performance and innovative platform suggest it is currently undervalued. The TIKR Valuation Model supports this outlook, forecasting continued earnings expansion based on robust revenue growth rates and operating margins.
